title: Hook runner 取消与输出上限未形成进程树边界
description: Hook command 不接收 AbortSignal；Windows 输出超限只 kill shell，且 finish 后 data listener 仍可继续积累，可能留下孙进程或突破 1 MiB 上限。
fix_direction: 为 runCommandHook 贯穿 AbortSignal，并让 abort、timeout、output-limit 共享可等待的 terminateProcessTree；settled 后停止收集并销毁 pipe。

context.suggested_fix: 复用 SmartSearch runner 的进程树终止策略，增加 bounded byte collector 与 abort/overflow regression tests。

title: Teammate execution 缺少有界输出和可靠进程回收
description: Teammate runner 的 abort/timeout 只 kill 直接 child，stdout/stderr 与 malformed JSONL 可无界增长，structured output 读取或解析失败被空 catch 吞掉。
fix_direction: 统一进程树终止、byte budget、bounded tail 与 structured-output typed failure；用 close 状态而非 child.killed 做 escalation。

context.suggested_fix: 添加 Windows tree-kill、增量 UTF-8 decoder、stdout/stderr ring buffer，以及 invalid structured output 回归测试。
context.notes: 文件当前存在用户未提交改动，故未在本次 SmartSearch 提交中直接修改。

title: Teammate 配置 TUI 的窄宽度返回与持久化不耐演进
description: Control Center 在宽度小于 20 时 nested Esc 直接关闭；model routing 保存会丢弃未知字段并直接覆盖正式 JSON，升级或中断时可能丢配置。
fix_direction: 让极窄宽度沿用 editor back-navigation；读取时保留原始 root，保存仅 patch 已知字段，并使用同目录 temp + rename。

context.suggested_fix: 同步修复 model-mapping-overlay.ts 与 models/model-routing.ts，增加宽度 1..120、unknown-key round-trip、write failure tests。
context.notes: 相关文件当前存在用户未提交改动，故路由为 issue。

title: Runtime deep import 与 wildcard peer 缺少协议锁定
description: pi-maestro-flow 对 teammate 使用 caret 并 deep-import src，Pi host peers 使用 optional wildcard，pi-internals 还 deep-load dist 路径；PATH fallback 和 AGENTS 资源也缺少版本或 hash handshake。
fix_direction: 对内部 deep import 采用 exact dependency 或公开 entrypoint；为 host/runtime 加 protocol/schema/version handshake，并对 fallback 明确告警。

context.suggested_fix: 分阶段收紧 teammate dependency、Pi peer range 与 deep-import contract；为 bundled resources 增加 build-time manifest/hash。
context.notes: 合并 syntax/semantic 层 10 个同类 risk，避免重复 issue。

title: maestro-flow commit-pinned install lacks prepared dist
description: Fresh install of pinned maestro-flow commit 84ae24f8ed9a12cac3b5c69ea3428840a0a58e1b fails before the Session/Run CLI can start because the Git source package omits dist/src/utils/wasm-relaunch.js. The source package exposes prepublishOnly but no prepare or prepack step, and neither GitHub Releases nor workflow runs provide a built asset. Registry maestro-flow@0.5.50 is not a compatible substitute because it exposes the legacy run contract and lacks create/brief/check/complete/cancel, parent_run_id, and seal-session behavior.
fix_direction: Publish a prepared artifact for the pinned commit or add a Git-install-safe prepare/prepack pipeline that builds and includes the complete dist tree without weakening the source lock.

context.suggested_fix: From a clean consumer, install the exact pinned Git commit and run node node_modules/maestro-flow/bin/maestro.js run create --help. Ensure dist/src/utils/wasm-relaunch.js exists and verify create/brief/check/complete/cancel plus parent_run_id on the installed artifact.
context.notes: Reproduction currently throws ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for dist/src/utils/wasm-relaunch.js. Symlink, registry downgrade, or skipped lifecycle assertions are explicitly not acceptable verification.

issue_history.note: Issue created from Odyssey packaging verification

title: PlanStore refresh and release retain check-then-act TOCTOU
description: PlanStore removePendingIfOwned, refreshLock, releaseLock, and stale reclaim perform an ownership/token check followed by a separate filesystem write, rename, or directory removal. A concurrent reclaim and reacquire can occur between those operations, allowing an old owner to overwrite or delete a new owner's lock or pending approval even though PID birth identity now prevents simple PID-reuse deadlocks.
fix_direction: Introduce a filesystem ownership guard with an atomic compare-and-swap or generation-scoped directory protocol that makes pending cleanup, refresh, reclaim, and release conditional on the same immutable claim, including crash recovery and cross-platform behavior.

context.suggested_fix: Design a token- or epoch-named claim directory protocol so refresh cannot replace another generation and cleanup/release removes only the caller's immutable claim. Add deterministic two-owner interleaving tests for remove-after-reclaim, refresh-after-reclaim, reclaim-after-reacquire, and release-after-reclaim.
context.notes: Odyssey syntax, semantic, and focused explore scans independently confirmed four check-then-act points. The correct fix requires a new filesystem guard and crash-recovery protocol rather than a local patch.

feedback.content: GENERALIZE expanded the scope from refresh/release to removePendingIfOwned and reclaim rename interleavings.

issue_history.note: Issue created from Odyssey sibling discovery

title: Teammate long-session registries and control UI retain stale state
description: Successful sleeping agents remain in activeRuns and namedAgents without count or TTL eviction, while Control Center active-agent data is a one-time snapshot and normal shutdown does not explicitly clear the widget context. Long sessions can retain unbounded agent records and show stale status after lifecycle changes.
fix_direction: Define named-agent retention semantics separately from ephemeral workers; add bounded count/TTL eviction for retired ephemeral records, live refresh for Control Center snapshots, and explicit widget dispose/null on every shutdown path.

context.suggested_fix: Add a retention policy with tests for thousands of completed ephemeral agents, named wake/resume preservation, overlay refresh after start/complete, and no UI updates after shutdown.
context.notes: Structural scan classified this as a cross-cutting lifecycle issue rather than a safe local patch.

issue_history.note: Issue created from Odyssey structural discovery

title: Goal projection publishes live state before durable persistence
description: Several Goal reconciliation and lifecycle paths assign the global activeGoal before appendEntry persistence and UI status updates finish. If persistence or a status setter throws, memory, durable history, and UI can diverge, unlike Todo's detached candidate and persist-first publish transaction.
fix_direction: Introduce a detached Goal candidate transaction that validates and persists the next goal and status before publishing activeGoal, with rollback-safe lifecycle fencing and deterministic injected-failure tests.

context.suggested_fix: Centralize Goal mutation in collect-next -> persist entry/status -> publish global state, then migrate create/reconcile/pause/complete paths incrementally.
context.notes: Routed as an issue because a local assignment reorder would not safely cover verifier timers, loop ownership, recovery, and statusline side effects.

issue_history.note: Issue created from Odyssey structural discovery

title: Config stores lack cross-process OCC and workspace containment
description: Permission settings, Hook trust, Smart Search config, and teammate model routing use independent read-modify-write stores without a shared revision or cross-process lock. Concurrent processes can lose unrelated updates. Permission local settings also derive .pi paths without proving the target remains inside the workspace when .pi is a directory symlink.
fix_direction: Adopt a shared revisioned JSON store protocol with unique private temp files, optimistic compare-and-swap or per-file lock, realpath containment for workspace-owned stores, crash recovery, and unknown-field preservation.

context.suggested_fix: Build one reusable JSON mutation primitive, then migrate permission settings, Hook trust, Smart Search config, and teammate model routing with multi-process interleaving and symlink-boundary tests.
context.notes: The current DISCOVER fix only addresses same-process serialization and temp-file collisions for permission/trust writes; cross-process OCC and containment remain intentionally routed here.

issue_history.note: Issue created from Odyssey cross-store discovery

title: Subprocess runners lack one cross-platform termination contract
description: Hook, LSP, Smart Search POSIX, and other CLI runners use different timeout, output-bound, AbortSignal, and process-tree termination behaviors. Some paths kill only the direct child, allowing shell or language-server descendants to survive cancellation, timeout, shutdown, or output overflow.
fix_direction: Extract a shared subprocess controller with once-only settlement, byte-bounded collectors, AbortSignal, timeout, Windows taskkill tree handling, POSIX process groups, escalation, and listener/timer cleanup.

context.suggested_fix: Migrate Hook runner, LSP shutdown, Smart Search, and Workflow CLI adapter to the same tested controller; cover descendant survival, ignored SIGTERM, overflow, abort, and double-settle races on Windows and POSIX.
context.notes: Hook runner is also represented by ISS-20260715-001; this issue captures the broader cross-runner architectural gap discovered by structural and historical scans.

issue_history.note: Issue created from Odyssey process-runner discovery

title: Flow 0.4.10 release depends on unpublished teammate 0.4.5
description: pi-maestro-flow 0.4.10 correctly pins pi-maestro-teammate 0.4.5, but the registry does not yet contain teammate 0.4.5. Local dual-tarball verification proves the code contract but not independent registry resolution; publishing Flow first would produce an uninstallable release.
fix_direction: Publish and verify pi-maestro-teammate 0.4.5 first, then publish pi-maestro-flow 0.4.10 and run a fresh registry-only consumer smoke without local tarballs or symlinks.

context.suggested_fix: Release order: teammate 0.4.5 -> npm view/install/export smoke -> Flow 0.4.10 -> registry-only packed consumer Session/Run smoke.
context.notes: This is a release sequencing gate, not a reason to weaken the exact dependency or reuse already-published 0.4.4.

issue_history.note: Issue created from Odyssey registry verification
